ogTitle: Hamlyn Terrace vs Hampden Hall: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hamlyn Terrace and Hampden Hall.
heading: Hamlyn Terrace vs Hampden Hall — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hamlyn Terrace, New South Wales and Hampden Hall, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hampden Hall has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Hamlyn Terrace (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hamlyn Terrace or Hampden Hall safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hamlyn Terrace has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Hampden Hall scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hamlyn Terrace scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Hampden Hall scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hamlyn Terrace or Hampden Hall?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hampden Hall has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
